concepts.name: Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A variable whose value is a numerical outcome of a random phenomenon.

concepts.name: Discrete Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A random variable that can only take a finite or countably infinite number of values.

concepts.name: Continuous Random Variable
concepts.short_definition: A random variable that can take any value within a given range.

concepts.name: Probability Mass Function
concepts.short_definition: The function that gives the probability that a discrete random variable is exactly equal to some value.

concepts.name: Probability Density Function
concepts.short_definition: The function whose integral over a given range gives the probability that a continuous random variable falls within that range.

concepts.name: Cumulative Distribution Function
concepts.short_definition: The function that gives the probability that a random variable is less than or equal to a certain value.

concepts.name: Expected Value
concepts.short_definition: The weighted average of all possible values of a random variable.

concepts.name: Variance
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the spread or dispersion of a random variable's values around its expected value.

concepts.name: Standard Deviation
concepts.short_definition: The square root of the variance, providing a measure of spread in the same units as the random variable.

concepts.name: Bernoulli Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A discrete distribution for a random variable with two possible outcomes (success/failure).

concepts.name: Binomial Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A discrete distribution for the number of successes in a fixed number of independent Bernoulli trials.

concepts.name: Poisson Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A discrete distribution for the number of events occurring in a fixed interval of time or space.

concepts.name: Geometric Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A discrete distribution for the number of Bernoulli trials needed to get the first success.

concepts.name: Uniform Distribution (Discrete)
concepts.short_definition: A discrete distribution where all possible outcomes have equal probability.

concepts.name: Uniform Distribution (Continuous)
concepts.short_definition: A continuous distribution where all values within a given interval have equal probability density.

concepts.name: Exponential Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A continuous distribution for the time until an event occurs in a Poisson process.

concepts.name: Normal Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A symmetric, bell-shaped continuous distribution that is fundamental in statistics.

concepts.name: Standard Normal Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A normal distribution with a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1.

concepts.name: Central Limit Theorem
concepts.short_definition: States that the distribution of sample means approaches a normal distribution as sample size increases.

concepts.name: Joint Probability Distribution
concepts.short_definition: Describes the probabilities of two or more random variables taking on specific values or ranges.

concepts.name: Marginal Probability Distribution
concepts.short_definition: The probability distribution of a single random variable from a set of random variables.

concepts.name: Conditional Probability Distribution
concepts.short_definition: The probability distribution of a random variable given that another random variable has taken a specific value.

concepts.name: Covariance
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how much two random variables change together.

concepts.name: Correlation Coefficient
concepts.short_definition: A standardized measure of the linear relationship between two random variables.

concepts.name: Independence of Random Variables
concepts.short_definition: Two random variables are independent if the outcome of one does not affect the outcome of the other.

concepts.name: Moment Generating Function
concepts.short_definition: A function used to derive moments of a probability distribution.

concepts.name: Chebyshev's Inequality
concepts.short_definition: Provides a bound on the probability that a random variable deviates from its mean.

concepts.name: Law of Large Numbers
concepts.short_definition: States that the average of the results obtained from a large number of trials should be close to the expected value.

concepts.name: Multinomial Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A generalization of the binomial distribution for more than two possible outcomes.

concepts.name: Gamma Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A continuous distribution often used to model waiting times or the sum of exponential random variables.

concepts.name: Beta Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A continuous distribution defined on the interval [0, 1], often used to model probabilities or proportions.

concepts.name: Chi-Squared Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A continuous distribution that arises from the sum of squared standard normal random variables.

concepts.name: Cauchy Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A continuous distribution with heavy tails and undefined expected value.

concepts.name: Dirichlet Distribution
concepts.short_definition: A multivariate continuous distribution that is a generalization of the Beta distribution.

concepts.name: Random Vector
concepts.short_definition: A vector whose components are random variables.

concepts.name: Stochastic Process
concepts.short_definition: A collection of random variables indexed by time or another parameter.

concepts.name: Markov Property
concepts.short_definition: The future state of a process depends only on the present state, not on the past.

concepts.name: Stationary Process
concepts.short_definition: A stochastic process whose statistical properties do not change over time.

concepts.name: Ergodic Process
concepts.short_definition: A stationary process where time averages converge to ensemble averages.
